text: Last class you learned to ask polite, indirect questions to get information.
Today you learn how to make polite requests when you travel for work.
Boarding pass
Gate
Aisle seat
Luggage
Check-in
Departure
Customs
Reservation
Front desk
Itinerary
Could I have an aisle seat, please?
Could you check my luggage to Madrid?
I would like to confirm my reservation.
Would it be possible to get a late check-out?
Good morning. Could I check in for the flight to Madrid, please?
Of course. Could I see your passport and your reservation?
Here you are. Could I have an aisle seat, and I would like to check one bag.
No problem. Your luggage is checked through to Madrid. Here is your boarding pass.
Thank you. Could you tell me where the departure gate is?
Gate twenty-two. Please go through to customs now; boarding starts in forty minutes.
Could you tell me where the departure gate is?

Hi Simone, this is Daniel. I am calling about your business trip to Madrid for the contract signing. Here is your itinerary. Your flight leaves on Tuesday morning, so please get to the airport early. At check-in, ask for an aisle seat if you prefer one, and remember you have one bag to check, so keep your boarding pass and your passport ready. When you land, go through customs first, then take a taxi to the hotel. I made a reservation under your name, so at the front desk just confirm the booking and ask for a quiet room. The closing meeting is on Wednesday at ten. If anything changes, call me. Have a safe trip, and talk soon.

Hello, this is Simone. I am recording my travel checklist for the Madrid trip, so I do not forget anything. First, at the airport, I will check in and say: could I have an aisle seat, please? I have one bag, so I will ask them to check my luggage. Then I will keep my boarding pass safe and find the departure gate. After I land, I will go through customs and take a taxi. At the hotel front desk, I would like to confirm my reservation, and I will ask: could I have a quiet room and a late check-out? Finally, I will review my itinerary for the closing meeting on Wednesday. I think I am ready for the trip.

Daniel leaves a voice note with the itinerary for the Madrid trip. Simone packs her bag and gets to the airport early. At the check-in desk, Simone asks for an aisle seat and checks her luggage. She goes through customs and takes a taxi to the hotel. At the front desk, Simone confirms her reservation and asks for a quiet room.
